title: Real-World Gallery
subtitle: Builder pattern in production code you use every day

examples.title: Java StringBuilder
examples.description: The most used Builder in Java. Append strings step by step, call toString() to get the final immutable String. StringBuilder is mutable, String is immutable.
examples.lesson: StringBuilder is the OG Builder. Mutable staging area -> immutable product. Every Java developer uses Builder daily without realizing it.

examples.title: Java Stream API
examples.description: Stream operations (filter, map, sorted) build a pipeline step by step. collect() or toList() is the build() call that produces the final result.
examples.lesson: Stream pipeline = lazy Builder. Intermediate operations configure. Terminal operation = build(). Nothing happens until you 'build' the result.

examples.title: OkHttp Request.Builder
examples.description: Every HTTP request in OkHttp is built via Builder. URL, headers, body, method -- configured step by step, validated at build().
examples.lesson: OkHttp processes millions of requests. Every one is built via Builder. If it's good enough for OkHttp, it's good enough for your code.

examples.title: Protocol Buffers (Protobuf) Builder
examples.description: Every Protobuf message in Google's ecosystem is created via Builder. Messages are immutable by design. Builder is the only way to create them.
examples.lesson: Google uses Builder for ALL inter-service communication via Protobuf. Billions of messages per day, every one built via Builder. Immutability at Google scale.

examples.title: Lombok @Builder
examples.description: Lombok generates Builder boilerplate from an annotation. Convenient but missing validation -- customize build() for real Builder pattern.
examples.lesson: Lombok gives you Builder SYNTAX. For Builder SEMANTICS (validation, computed fields), customize build() by partially defining the inner class.

examples.title: Java 11 HttpClient & HttpRequest
examples.description: Java 11's built-in HTTP client uses Builder for both the client configuration and individual requests. Two-level Builder.
examples.lesson: Even the JDK uses Builder extensively. HttpClient.newBuilder() and HttpRequest.newBuilder() are the standard Java way to make HTTP calls.
